text: When designing REST APIs, it is important to follow best practices to ensure they are easy to use and maintain. First, endpoints should represent resources clearly, using nouns like `/users` or `/orders`. Use HTTP methods such as GET and POST to perform actions. Always include appropriate status codes in responses; for example, `200` for success, `404` for not found, and `500` for server errors. Good documentation is essential—it helps developers understand how to use your API quickly. Finally, consider versioning your API, such as `/v1/users`, so changes don't break existing clients. These practices make your API reliable and developer-friendly.
